title: General Audience Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a general audience abstract that clearly and concisely summarizes the proposed research project in non-technical language. The abstract should: 1. Briefly introduce the research problem related to melanoma and its significance. 2. Explain the project's objectives and approach in layman's terms. 3. Highlight the potential impact of the research on melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, or treatment. 4. Be easily understandable by individuals without scientific expertise. 5. Adhere to the character limit of 2000 characters (approximately 285 words).
search_queries: how to write a general audience abstract

title: Technical Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a technical abstract that summarizes the proposed research project for a scientific audience. The abstract should: 1. Clearly state the research question and specific aims related to melanoma. 2. Briefly describe the experimental design and methods to be used. 3. Summarize expected results and their potential significance. 4. Use appropriate scientific terminology. 5. Adhere to the character limit of 2000 characters (approximately 285 words).
search_queries: how to write a technical research abstract

title: Project Description
generation_instructions: Write a detailed project description that comprehensively outlines the proposed research on melanoma. The project description should: 1. Provide a clear and concise background of the research problem and its significance in the field of melanoma. 2. State the specific aims and objectives of the project. 3. Present preliminary data supporting the feasibility and rationale of the proposed research. 4. Describe the experimental design and methods in detail, including controls and statistical analysis plan. 5. Discuss potential challenges and alternative approaches. 6. Explain the potential clinical impact of the research findings. 7. Highlight the innovative aspects of the research. 8. Adhere to the 5-page limit (approximately 1735 words).

title: Clinical Trials Protocol Synopsis
generation_instructions: Write a brief clinical trials protocol synopsis that summarizes the key elements of the proposed clinical trial for melanoma. The synopsis should: 1. Briefly describe the study design, including the patient population, intervention, and control groups. 2. State the primary and secondary outcome measures. 3. Outline the statistical analysis plan. 4. Provide a timeline with key milestones, including IRB approval, patient accrual, and completion of analyses. 5. Address ethical considerations and data safety monitoring. 6. Include a dissemination plan for clinical trial information. 7. Adhere to the 5-page limit (approximately 1735 words).
search_queries: IRB approval process for clinical trials
data safety monitoring in clinical trials
